executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 379,861.30 inc VAT (349,000.23 ex VAT) for the week ending 20 June 2026.
Overall store margin is reported at 17.0%, which is under pressure given the department-level data showing significant stock movement anomalies across multiple categories.
Total waste for the week stands at 5,407.66, representing a waste percentage of 1.55% of ex-VAT sales, which is elevated and requires immediate attention.

The department P&L total shows a combined departmental margin of 113.22% on sales of 120,698.94, driven by negative cost of sales (-15,959.48) across several categories, indicating significant stock valuation discrepancies or data integrity issues.
Produce is the highest-grossing department at 40,998.04 in sales and also the single largest source of waste at 1,684.83, representing a material margin risk.
Fish waste of 1,291.08 is disproportionately high relative to its sales base of 5,406.12, equating to approximately 23.9% of Fish sales lost to waste.
Provisions waste of 701.94 against sales of 25,907.49 and a scan margin of 19.91% warrants investigation to protect what should be a high-volume, stable department.

Several fresh departments show closing stock values substantially higher than opening stock, which is distorting cost of sales figures and must be verified for accuracy before any margin conclusions are drawn.
biggest_wins: Provisions delivered 25,870.77 in gross profit on sales of 25,907.49, achieving a near-100% reported margin (99.86%), indicating strong throughput with minimal stockholding cost recognised this week.
Produce generated 32,707.85 in gross profit on sales of 40,998.04, representing the highest absolute profit contribution of any single department this week.

Poultry achieved sales of 12,781.98 with a gross profit of 14,040.34, and while the reported margin is inflated by stock movement, the scan margin of 16.84% and controlled waste of 48.27 indicate solid operational performance.

Deli posted sales of 9,617.54 with a gross profit of 9,100.62 (94.63% reported), and a scan margin of 39.82% which is the highest scan margin across all departments, suggesting strong recipe and pricing discipline.

Beef delivered 22,421.33 in reported gross profit on sales of 14,236.06, and while the margin percentage is distorted by stock movements, the scan margin of 20.08% reflects a commercially sound category.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes show a closing stock of 127,896.58 versus an opening stock of 114,751.02, producing a negative cost of sales of -13,145.56 and a reported margin of 267.91% against a scan margin of 12.36%. This is a significant data integrity flag and must be audited immediately as it is masking true performance.

Beef closing stock of 9,554.63 against an opening stock of 1,369.36 results in a negative cost of sales of -8,185.27 and a reported margin of 157.5% versus a scan margin of 20.08%. A stock count verification is required urgently.

Fish waste of 1,291.08 on sales of 5,406.12 equates to approximately 23.9% of Fish sales written off as waste, making it the highest waste-to-sales ratio of any department and a critical margin threat.

Produce waste of 1,684.83 is the largest absolute waste figure in the store. With no closing stock recorded for the week, the cost of sales calculation relies entirely on opening stock, reducing confidence in the 79.78% reported margin.

Deli waste of 484.54 on sales of 9,617.54 (approximately 5.0% of Deli sales) is high for a department where production planning should be controllable. Combined with a scan margin of 39.82%, excessive waste is significantly eroding potential profitability.

Provisions waste of 701.94 on sales of 25,907.49 (approximately 2.7% of Provisions sales) is above acceptable thresholds for an ambient-heavy department and needs a review of ordering patterns and date rotation.

Lamb and Pork both show closing stocks materially higher than their opening stocks, generating negative cost of sales figures and heavily distorted margins (192.19% and 147.58% respectively). These figures do not reflect true trading performance and require stock count validation.
Bacon and Loose SRP reports a scan margin of only 10.87%, the lowest of any department, indicating potential pricing, shrink, or markdown issues that need to be investigated at line level.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Reported margin of 267.91% is not credible against a scan margin of 12.36%. Closing stock has increased by 13,145.56 week-on-week, creating a negative COGS. A full stock count and purchase reconciliation is required before this department's figures can be relied upon.

Beef: Closing stock of 9,554.63 versus opening of 1,369.36 distorts reported margin to 157.5% against a scan margin of 20.08%. Waste is well controlled at 69.05. Stock count accuracy must be confirmed.

Lamb: Reported margin of 192.19% against a scan margin of 27.11% reflects a closing stock of 1,507.63 against an opening of 260.86. Waste of 38.36 is reasonable for the sales volume of 1,352.41. Stock valuation needs review.

Pork: Similar stock inflation issue with closing of 1,148.07 versus opening of 382.24, inflating the reported margin to 147.58% against a scan margin of 24.25%. Waste of 41.28 is acceptable. Verify stock count.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Scan margin of 10.87% is the weakest across all departments. Reported margin of 128.5% is again driven by a negative cost of sales (-273.77). A pricing and shrink review at line level is recommended.

Poultry: Sales of 12,781.98 are strong and waste of 48.27 is well controlled. Scan margin of 16.84% is below the store average, suggesting room to improve through mix management or waste reduction at the margin.

Fish: Sales of 5,406.12 with waste of 1,291.08 is operationally unsustainable. The cost of sales is positive at 72.25, which combined with high waste means actual net margin is deeply compressed. Ordering volumes and production scheduling need immediate review.

Produce: Highest sales department at 40,998.04 and highest absolute waste at 1,684.83. No closing stock recorded this period impacts cost of sales reliability. Waste reduction in Produce should be the primary operational focus this week.

Deli: Scan margin of 39.82% is the strongest in the store, indicating good recipe and pricing structure. However, waste of 484.54 on sales of 9,617.54 (approximately 5.0%) is eroding this advantage. Production planning and end-of-day markdown procedures should be reviewed.

Provisions: Near-total gross profit of 25,870.77 on sales of 25,907.49 reflects a very low recognised cost this week. Waste of 701.94 is the second highest in the store in absolute terms and needs to be addressed through tighter date management and ordering discipline.
action_plan: Conduct a full stock count for Cigarettes immediately and reconcile against purchase records for the week. The 13,145.56 increase in closing stock against a 12.36% scan margin is unexplained and must be resolved before next week's P&L is accepted.

Verify Beef, Lamb, and Pork closing stock counts. All three departments show closing stocks significantly higher than opening stocks, generating implausible negative cost of sales figures. Department managers to confirm counts were taken correctly and at correct cost values.

Investigate Fish waste of 1,291.08 urgently. Review ordering quantities, delivery schedules, and daily production output. Implement a waste log review with the Fish counter manager and set a target to reduce Fish waste by a minimum of 30% in the coming week.

Review Produce ordering and waste management processes. At 1,684.83 in waste this week, the Produce department is the largest single contributor to total store waste. Introduce daily waste reporting and consider adjusting order quantities on highest-waste lines.

Review Deli production planning to address waste of 484.54. Introduce or reinforce a last-hour markdown programme for end-of-day Deli lines to convert potential waste into discounted sales. Review production schedules against actual hourly sales patterns.

Investigate Provisions waste of 701.94. Pull a waste report by product line within Provisions to identify the primary contributors. Implement date rotation audits and review minimum order quantities on lines with short shelf life.

Review Bacon and Loose SRP at line level to understand the 10.87% scan margin, the lowest in the store. Check for pricing errors, unrecorded shrink, or markdown volumes that may be dragging the scan margin below expectations.

Ensure Produce closing stock is captured and recorded correctly next week. The absence of a closing stock figure for Produce this period has compromised the reliability of the department cost of sales and margin calculations.

Reconcile total department purchases against the reported zero purchase figure in the department P&L summary. All departments show no purchase values recorded, which is contributing to the distorted cost of sales calculations across the board. Confirm with the accounts team that purchases have been correctly posted.

Brief all fresh department managers on the current waste percentage of 1.55% (5,407.66 total) and set individual department waste targets for the coming week. Prioritise Fish, Produce, Deli, and Provisions as the four highest-impact areas.

Review Poultry scan margin of 16.84% against the department's strong sales performance of 12,781.98. Assess whether the category mix can be shifted toward higher-margin lines, and confirm that all promotions are correctly set up at point of sale.

Schedule a full department P&L review meeting with the store manager and relevant department heads for early in the coming week to address the stock valuation anomalies across Cigarettes, Beef, Lamb, Pork, and Bacon before the figures are finalised.
